_comment: Ground truth for benchmark/fixtures/*.c, built from manual review of a real `maishac scan` run (all 3 analyzers, include_paths=[benchmark/stubs]) on 2026 test day. Categories: 'seeded' = a defect deliberately planted in the fixture to test recall. 'known_true_extra_rules' = rule IDs that legitimately, correctly fire on this code as a side effect of realistic patterns (multiple returns, missing header prototypes, implicit pointer/int conversions, etc.) that were NOT deliberately seeded but are not false positives either -- allow-listed by rule id rather than exact line since they're incidental. 'known_false_positives' = specific (file, line, rule) triples manually confirmed to be analyzer mistakes, not real defects. Anything found that is in neither list is 'unclassified' and must be manually triaged before being called a false positive.

_known_true_extra_rules_notes.MISRA-C:2012 Dir 4.6 (04_memory_and_sign: cmp_int's parameters are 'const void *', matched loosely by the native basic-type heuristic; a genuine, if incidental, Dir 4.6-adjacent hit.
_known_true_extra_rules_notes.clang-tidy:clang-analyzer-security.insec: Clang's insecureAPI family flags memcpy/memset/strcpy/sprintf/scanf UNCONDITIONALLY, even on already-safely-bounded call sites (confirmed: it fires twice on 07_clean_reference.c's provably-safe memcpy/memset). This is a real characteristic of the underlying engine, not a Maisha bug -- but it means even MISRA-compliant, bounds-checked code will need per-project suppressions/deviations for these functions, same as Maisha's own native BANNED_CALLS policy.
_known_true_extra_rules_notes.clang-tidy:clang-diagnostic-error: Only ever appears in the WITHOUT --include comparison run, one per file with an unresolved system header. Confirms the include-path fix: this class disappears entirely once --include is supplied.

seeded.note: printf stdio in production (native, call site)

false_positive_traps.why: bounded memcpy with explicit length clamp and manual null-termination; must NOT be flagged as an unbounded copy
returns a pointer to static storage, not dynamically allocated or dangling; must NOT be flagged as a memory-safety issue

_fixed_false_positives.note: g_log_buf is a fixed array sized by a compile-time macro (LOG_BUF_SIZE), not a variable-length array. FIXED this cycle: native.py's VLA check now skips ALL_CAPS array-size identifiers (near-universal macro-constant naming convention). No longer fires here.

seeded.note: basic type 'int' instead of stdint type

false_positive_traps.why: constant-offset register-bank pointer arithmetic is standard driver practice; MISRA 18.4 must not fire (it correctly doesn't -- but MISRA 11.4, int-to-pointer conversion, legitimately does fire here, which is a DIFFERENT and genuinely correct rule, not a false positive; see known_true_extra_rules)

02_register_driver.c.note: Dir 4.6 also incidentally, correctly fires on lines 45 and 84 (uart_send_byte / uart_self_test both declared returning bare 'int'). Not deliberately seeded as separate cases but genuinely correct -- counted as incidental true positives, not false positives.

false_positive_traps.why: a DIFFERENT function, with a multi-line signature, that legitimately calls retry_backoff_ms once -- the historical Maisha bug (fixed this cycle) misattributed this call site to retry_backoff_ms itself and reported false recursion
an ordinary bounded for-loop with no recursion or missing braces -- must not be flagged by an over-eager heuristic
a plain, safe unsigned <= comparison against a named parameter -- must not be flagged

05_float_and_int_edge_cases.c.note: check_shutdown_threshold (lines 58-63) is the README's sentinel-cast trap. No native/cppcheck/clang-tidy rule is expected to catch the SEMANTIC bug (that -1 becomes UINT32_MAX); this fixture instead documents that a rescan-only ('analyzer_only') policy would consider it clean. The behavioral risk is exercised end-to-end against the verification gate in the synthetic firmware module, not here.

seeded.note: genuine braceless if body, positioned next to preprocessor noise
#undef inside clamp_reading (still a real #undef, rule is intentionally broad)

false_positive_traps.why: if-header's real body IS braced, just on the far side of an #if/#else/#endif block -- the exact FreeRTOS-derived false-positive pattern the MISRA 15.6 preprocessor-blindness fix targets

if-header's real body IS braced, on the far side of #ifdef/#undef/#endif -- must not be flagged, and the #undef itself must not confuse the brace check (it legitimately triggers 20.5 as its own separate, correct finding)
deliberately well-written: fixed-width types, bounded copies, no dynamic memory, no banned calls, always-braced, single-exit functions, no float equality. Expected near-zero substantive findings.

_fixed_false_positives.note: uint8_t data[RING_BUFFER_CAPACITY] inside a struct -- same class as 01_string_handling.c line 15. FIXED this cycle, see that file's note.
